patient_message: I've been dealing with persistent, symmetrical numbness and tingling in my feet for WEEKS now. The sensation of pain and temperature in feet feels reduced, and my vibration sense is notably impaired. I also notice mild gait instability and occasional paresthesia in the ulnar distribution, which I didn't have before. No new meds or major lifestyle changes, but my blood sugars have been poorly controlled—A1c has been around 9. I had nerve conduction studies recently, which pointed toward diabetic sensorimotor polyneuropathy, but symptoms haven't improved despite strict glucose monitoring. This ongoing numbness is becoming pretty frustrating, especially since I've read that worse glycemic control can worsen neuropathy. Would appreciate guidance on next steps or possible treatment adjustments, as this is the third time I’ve written about this and I feel like nobody really takes it seriously. Thanks.
